entry.title: How to set an Aactive ATC run in ATC result browser in Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have a question I have recently started using the ATC possibilities in Eclipse I am connected to an S4 System and was wonderrring how this section in Eclipse gets filled 

Is it possible to run an ATC i the TCODE ATC in the GUI and get the results over here?

Thanks you for reading.

entry.title: 1H 2024 Learning - Background Job for Adding Library Items
entry.content: Can you please explain in which case it makes sense to activate this setting?

entry.title: Re: Strategic and Operational Workforce Planning with SAP Best Practices for SAP SuccessFactors and
entry.content:
@RF-SAP 

Hi,

I understand that there is PMGM module in SuccessFactors (performance and goal management).

Do you have idea whether the data / dimensions required for strategic planning (strategic aims, objectives, opportunities, and challenges at the business area and employee level) etc. are available in the PMGM module. 

I intend to acquire the data from SFSF's  PMGM module into SAP Analytics Cloud to implement strategic planning workflow

entry.title: Can your Non Profit Organization Harness the Power of AI?
entry.content:
The world around us is changing at a rapid pace. Powerful trends – amplified and accelerated humanitarian disasters are creating a pressurized environment for many Nonprofit organizations.  Your role has never been more critical than today in addressing social, environmental, and humanitarian issues.  Many of you have limited resources, and your organizations are experiencing employee and donor burnout, and a myriad of other unique issues depending on your operational context.  Donors expect more and trust less; and your organization must do more with less.  Outdated business applications and isolated systems are making it difficult to seize new opportunities and meet changing requirements.  However, new AI capabilities have massive potential - if you're able to harness them. 

Business applications with AI deeply embedded (Business AI) are now able to better automate tasks such as accounting, human resources, reporting, and mission-critical activities. Business AI marks a paradigm shift in how organizations can garner insights from data, automate, and solve issues. In fact, Forrester researchers predict that enterprise AI initiatives will boost productivity and creative problem-solving by 50%, and IDC predicts that by 2026, 90% of G2000 organizations will augment operational roles with automation technology, unlocking a 30% increase in worker efficiency.

AI is already a central, integrated capability of enterprise business applications, and use cases are starting to emerge from Nonprofits who are using Business AI to become more effective.  Here are three examples:   

WEConnect International is a global network that connects women-owned businesses to qualified buyers around the world. The organization needed to be transformed to meet their requirements for user-friendly, secure, and scalable processes. Member buyers using the system needed to be able to search for qualified women-owned businesses quickly using keywords, and often WEConnect international employees were required to manage the searches.  Due to the global nature of the organization, it was also a problem that women-owned businesses could only register using English language, and multiple forms and systems which were not secure were necessary for uploading documents during registration. WEConnect use Intelligent Robotic Process Automation to centralize and simplify the process for women-owned businesses to register, upload documents, and get certified, and to make it easier for buyers to quickly search for a product or service from qualified businesses. 

As a 100% volunteer-driven, nonprofit NGO, Essen für Alle (Food for All, Switzerland) sought an efficient method to optimize its focus on feeding the food insecure. It required a method that helps ensure continuous volunteer coverage across all tasks, while effectively monitoring and managing food donations and distributions. Their volunteer shift planning and schedule management system captures, assesses, and reports volunteer data, empowering administrators to identify gaps and flexibly schedule shifts. Simultaneously, the recipient management system helps facilitate smooth distribution management through AI-enabled document extraction to scan passports, IDs and driver’s licenses and automate the registration process. When integrated together, these systems drive better shift planning and smooth distribution with more-effective tracking and control of food resources. 

Diakone Nord Nord Ost is dedicated to helping people in all life circumstances in more than 100 facilities across Northern Germany. The nonprofit started using Business AI by leveraging a bundle of cloud microservices to automate and simplify the order-to-cash process in accounts receivable with machine learning.  The solution helps reduce manual post-processing of incoming bank statements by applying machine learning to match open receivables to incoming bank statement items or lockbox files, to match supplier invoices to outgoing supplier-initiated payments, and to identify customer accounts for incoming payments.  Introduction of these intelligent capabilities has led to a significant improvement in employee satisfaction by reducing the need for repetitive tasks and enabling employees to focus on higher value activities, resulting in more variation and productivity in their day-to-day work.

These examples provide a glimpse into what is being accomplished today. Here are some additional use cases that Business AI capabilities can provide.     

Nonprofits can utilize Business AI built into finance applications with AI-powered insights, recommendations, and built-in automation.  Business AI leverages advanced algorithms and machine learning to analyze large amounts of data and generate insights into financial, grants, and operational data.  Organizations can use AI to identify patterns in donor behavior leading to more effective fundraising campaigns and increased donor retention. 

In addition, Nonprofits could use business integrity screening to scan large volumes of data in real-time with increased accuracy to detect and prevent fraud and errors. The software helps you identify anomalous activity quickly using flexible rule sets and predictive analysis that can help uncover potential fraud patterns. 

Business AI built into customer service applications can provide tailored solutions to stakeholders, making each interaction an opportunity to improve satisfaction and loyalty. Donors can engage with non-profit organizations using natural language and have simple inquiries resolved at the point of initial contact, without the need of personal interaction. Complex, higher-risk inquiries can be routed to employees who could then focus on providing effective, and personalized services.

The use of natural language (conversational analytics) is making it easier for users to make more refined queries on data.  Users can measure program effectiveness and demonstrate programmatic impact to donors and stakeholders by analyzing large datasets and surfacing the most pertinent insights.   

Virtual assistants embedded into business applications from HR to finance, procurement, marketing, and sales can transform the user experience – it’s like tapping your smartest colleague on the shoulder. Employees can ask a question in natural language and receive intelligent answers drawn from the data across the applications portfolio and third-party sources, retaining context. This not only reduces manual inputs but also ensures time savings, fewer errors, and enhanced efficiency through AI-driven recommendations.

It is always nice to collaborate on the art of the possible.  But where should you consider starting?  I recommend you consider adopting an incremental approach.

  • Start with small projects:  Test the feasibility and benefits of the technology and then scale as you become more comfortable.  Jump-start the use of AI by investing in pre-built solutions to test out narrow use cases.  
  • Collaborate: Include a cross functional team in decisions and roll-out to ensure the use of technology aligns with your mission and benefits the communities you serve.  Engage with other Nonprofit organizations to share knowledge and understand lessons learned. 
  • Safeguard:  Allow for learning and adjustment along the way, minimizing risks. Fully understand the systems limitations and ensure transparency and explainabilty in AI algorithms.  Ensure that the security capabilities provided by vendors ensure appropriate encryption and access controls and that you can adhere to applicable data protection regulations. 

Many Nonprofit organizations need to steer the organization through disruption to achieve the best outcomes which requires both cultural and technological transformation.  While the integration of AI and enterprise business applications capabilities are noteworthy, the real promise of Business AI for Nonprofits isn’t what it can do so much as the value it will create.    Adding capabilities using AI models pretrained on business-relevant data will inherently provide better outcomes. 

I encourage you to go visit the collection of articles on AI in the SAP Insights page or here in the community.     It may provide some good insights for your organizations road ahead.  Happy Reading.

entry.title: Re: Which SAP GUI for Windows as of release are adapted f...
entry.content: 7.50 will work. If you want to use new HTML Control use >= 7.70

entry.title: User password forgotten/reset
entry.content:
Dear all,

We have a CUA on Solution Manager 7.2 and we would like to provide the users with a "Password Reset" or "Password Forgotten" facility/link without installing SAP IDM product.

Is it any way to provide that though a link in the SAP WebUI (login screen) or SAPGUI or may be using a BAPI_USER_CHANGE called from a web service ?

Thanks a lot in advance

Max

entry.title: Re: 1H 2024 Learning Improvement - More detail about Impr...
entry.content:
Here are the details:
The suspend data for online content controls how progress on a learning session is stored and retrieved. We've increased the suspend data limit for SCORM 1.2 content to 64,000 characters to provide the additional storage capacity required for content progress to be successfully stored and retrieved.

Some eLearning authoring tools (like Articulate Rise) tend to try to store tons of data in the suspend date field. If they exceeded the previous limit of 4096 characters, the lesson would not save the user's place in the lesson. 

Increasing the limit will allow for longer lessons to be able to store the users' place in the lesson.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_RELEASE_INFORMATION/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/c151e1e747db4708a618236e4dc98512.html?parentHref=%2Fwhats-new%2F8fcf4960eea24f78b1d7613da406a885%3FProduct%3DLearning%26Version%3D1H%25202024%26locale%3Den-US&parentName=SAP%20SuccessFactors%20What%27s%20New

entry.title: Outlook Visit types to C4C
entry.content:
Hello experts,

does someone know if we can add the different Visit/ Appointment Types from C4C to the Outlook categories? So that I can create a Visit in Outlook with the type XY and it appears in C4C with the corresponding visit type.

At the moment, those visits from outlook have the visit type ''not assigned''. So the sales rep have to change the visit type in C4C.

 Thanks!

entry.title: CDS Compiler 4.8.0 rejects function & action return types declared as 'many of many'?!
entry.content:
Hello,

because of a failing deployment for a customer I found out that the latest (v4.8.0) CDS Compiler now rejects function/action return types that are declared as 'many of many'. This is outlined in the changelog.md file, which you can view on npmjs.com. While such a type may not be common in every piece of software, I firmly believe that this is a design choice that has to be made by the developer, not the cds compiler. Why was this change necessary?

Furthermore I find it very worrying that this kind of breaking change is released in a minor patch. Also, why would this change be made just now? Some insight into the thought process towards this would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: New Financial Accounting for Service Management in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Stefan,

Great blog. Thank you.

Will the aggregated type of reporting you describe in chapter 1 of this blog work equally well in the Service with advanced execution implementation? 
What is the integration mechanism between the Service orders and the PM order? Is it based on settlement or can the Service order reporting access the PM order line item detail?

Thank you
Patrick

entry.title: How to monitor SAP Cloud Integration jobs on solman?
entry.content:
Hello Support team,

We want to integrate the SAP Cloud Integration jobs into Solution Manager and we want to know how to do it. Will you have any documentation or SAP NOTE to support us? 

 

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to monitor SAP Cloud Integration jobs on solman?
entry.content:
Hello,

To monitor cloud products on Solution Manager is necessary a Cloud Connector configuration and after that configure the managed system with cloud connector.

 

Please check the documentation:
Cloud Connector configuration: https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/configuration

0002607632 - SAP Solution Manager 7.2 - Managed System Configuration for SAP Cloud Connector

 

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: WhatsApp Group of Kolkata and Exam
entry.content:
Hi Sandip,

The first round of sorting was done concerning the exams. If you have any specific questions please let us know. We are refraining discussing any other social media in community groups.

entry.title: How can I use a method from Fiori Launchpad in my RAP-based App?
entry.content:
How can I use a method from Fiori Launchpad in my RAP-based App?

I am creating an App to release Billing Block from Sales Orders. so Fiori Launchpad has an App called Manage Sales Order V2 (F3893) it has a removebillingblock method defined in C_SalesOrderManage behavior definition and I want to use it in my RAP-based App.
So what is the optimal way to call this method(action) and use it in my App.

P.s I tried to trace this method with Fiori Launchpad package, It took so long and was so complicated sequence.

-------------------------------------------------------

define behavior for C_SalesOrderManage alias SalesOrder
use etag
extensible
{
use action RemoveBillingBlock; 

}

SAP S/4HANA SD (Sales and Distribution)  ABAP Extensibility  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: How to delete an ABAP system from Cloud ALM Landscape Management?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support,

 

we want to delete/remove/unregister the ABAP System from the CloudALM.

 

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete an ABAP system from Cloud ALM Landscape...
entry.content:
Hello,

Please remove firstly the endpoints:

  • Check the following links to remove every configuration for all the SAP Cloud ALM scenarios:
    • How to remove Health Monitoring Configuration
    • How to delete components in Business Process Monitoring
    • How to remove configuration in Integration & Exception Monitoring (see Deleting the Monitoring Configuration)
    • How to remove Job Monitoring Configuration (see Configuration of events)
    • For Business Service Management
      • Please remove all the services from any Business Service in BSM tile.
    • For Real User Monitoring
      • Please turn it off in Real User Monitoring tile. This will set it to Not Configured
    • Intelligent Event Processing is removed once all the previous scenarios is removed.

 

Please check the documentation:

0003248215 - How to delete Service/Systems in SAP Cloud ALM

 

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Which SAP GUI for Windows as of release are adapted f...
entry.content:
Hello @JasonZhong 

The answer by @thomas_mller13 is perfectly correct. On top of that you should look at the note 147519 - Maintenance strategy / deadlines for SAP GUI for Windows / SAP GUI for Java and check SAP GUI support deadlines. SAP GUI 7.50 is not supported since  31st of March 2020, yeah that's 4 years already. 

As you are moving to a brand new system, consider upgrading SAP GUI too. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Not uploaded as binary/text type is unknown
entry.content:
Please refer to this answer;

https://ga.support.sap.com/dtp/viewer/index.html#/tree/3046/actions/45995:45996:50742:46000:53797

The unknown file types have to be maintained in .Ui5RepositoryTextFiles or .Ui5RepositoryBinaryFiles files, according to https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/a883327a82ef4cc792f3c1e7b7a48de8

John

entry.title: Re: Replace a Model in the Optimized (Unified) Experience
entry.content:
Hi @Tunir 

We have an acquired Classic Account planning model, which we would like to replace in stories with an otherwise identical acquired New Model with accounts and measures planning model.

The acquired Classic Account planning model was originally created via a BPC 11.X import connection (not a live connection, and not a writeback model). The acquired New Model with accounts and measures planning model will be created from scratch in SAC.

Is this case supported?

I ask because although it is an acquired model, there are limitations elsewhere due to the fact that it happened to be created based on a BPC 11.X import connection originally, e.g. in the use of predictive features. To me I don't see why the source of data should make any difference to an acquired model.

Thanks,

Hugh

entry.title: Re: Activating User Defaults in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for the blog post, is very informative.

I have a problem when trying to apply this.

I have entered a list of Collection Groups in "Additional Value" but when I go into a Foiori App none of the default values are pre-populated and is ignoring my list of default values.

Is there anything missing?

Thanks

Isabel

entry.title: Re: Create Customer Attributes for Elements of the Process Management in SolMan 7.2
entry.content: @Vivek_Hegde : were you able to manage the multiple attribute selection

entry.title: Re: user created a some materials with reference to other...
entry.content: One workaround would be go to MM01 and against the field "Copy from", place your mouse and use space bar so that, it will list out the latest material codes used.  Of course, multiple material codes would be listed out in MM01 and "Copy from" field is used in all the cases.

entry.title: Free Solution Experience Live Session: Deliver Real-World Results with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
Unveiling SAP Business AI's Potential at the Upcoming Solution Experience Live Session

In an era of continuous innovation and heightened competitiveness, AI's ability to enable smarter decisions and efficiencies on monumental scales has become essential. One such solution that helps businesses to harness the power of AI is SAP Business AI.

The upcoming Solution Experience Live Session: Deliver Real-World Results with SAP Business AI presents a one-of-a-kind opportunity for everyone to grasp the countless benefits and applications of SAP Business AI. Led by Dr. Philipp Herzig, Chief AI Officer at SAP, this one-hour virtual session on Tuesday, April 30, 2024, from 1:30 p.m. to 2:30 p.m. CEST, will expand upon the innovative capabilities of SAP Business AI.

 

The impact of AI on businesses today cannot be overstated. It is integral – not just in streamlining operational processes, but in offering insights and opportunities that were once unthinkable. Increasingly, businesses are making deeper strides into leveraging AI for corporate advantage – with SAP Business AI at the forefront of this change. With AI-skilled experts being the most sought-after professionals in the workforce, the challenge is to close the AI skills gap. SAP has made it it’s mission to make a variety of learning resources on the SAP Learning site available to equip anyone with the right tools to navigate today's AI-driven business environment.

Join us for the virtual session, collectively debunk the mysteries of AI, and tap into the potentials of SAP Business AI. The free-of-charge solution experience live session is open to everyone — mark your calendars and prepare to journey into a AI-empowered future.

Make sure to sign up today.

Want to discover live sessions beyond AI?

With a subscription to SAP Learning Hub, you can unlock further live sessions in other in-demand topics, such as SAP BTP, ABAP Cloud development and how to build apps with SAP Build. You can explore the full live session schedule and join the sessions of your liking with an SAP Learning Hub subscription.

entry.title: Re: Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info S...
entry.content: It seems to me that the "Edit Object" and "Add Objects" rights are denied for the root folder "Groups and Users". Check the top level security for “Users and Groups".

entry.title: The Role of SAP Business AI in the Chemical Industry. Overview
entry.content:
Welcome colleagues. Today I’d like to share the description of SAP high level positioning of the Business AI for the Chemicals and Process Industries.

SAP advantage: Business AI Unveiled

At SAP, our commitment to Business AI is underscored by three pillars: relevance, reliability, and responsibility. By embedding these principles into our enterprise applications and business technology solutions, we ensure that our clients receive future-proofed AI experiences tailored to their business context.

Want to know more? Please click here 

AI Impact on the Chemical Industry

To make it short: the advent of AI has revolutionized the Chemical Industry, enhancing efficiency across business processes, manufacturing operations, and decision-making frameworks. Through AI and Machine Learning (ML) algorithms, chemical companies can optimize manufacturing outcomes, refine demand forecasting accuracy, and elevate sales effectiveness - and these are only couple of examples we can provide, there is more below in this blog.

Survey Insights: AI's for Chemical Companies

Collaborating with esteemed industry communities like CEAC (Chemical Executive Advisory Council), ACC (American Chemistry Council) and others, we (SAP IBU Chemicals) have “distilled” the most important scenarios for chemical businesses. We have performed several surveys of AI experts within chemical companies has revealed key areas where AI promises maximum benefits.

From the common AI direction point these priorities are:

  • Help with decision making
  • Process & Routine Tasks Automation
  • Assistance in R&D and Recipe generation
  • Improve the Employee & Customer Experience using natural language

And, we have gathered scenarios prioritization according to respective business domains.

Customer Experience:

  • 360 Customers Review
  • Natural Language Assistance for Sales managers
  • Price Changes Impact Prediction

Procurement:

  • 360 Suppliers Review
  • Supplier Document Data Extraction
  • Contractors job description

Finance & Reporting:

  • Natural Language Assistance in Analytical Reporting
  • Finance Data Insights and Advisory

Supply Chain Management:

  • Demand Forecasting
  • Chemical Technology Process Optimization
  • Visual Inspection for Asset Maintenance & Quality Management

Governance & IT:

  • Master Data Management, Data enrichment and Quality
  • Natural Language User Assistance
  • Business Process Analysis
  • Code Generation and Analysis
  • R&D – Recipe Development Assistance

These scenarios represent the collective vision of industry experts and serve as a roadmap for integrating AI-driven solutions into the fabric of chemical businesses. By aligning our efforts with these prioritized scenarios, SAP aims to empower organizations to unlock new levels of efficiency, agility, and innovation in the ever-evolving landscape of the chemical industry.

How SAP responses on these prioritization?

Responding to industry demands, we have integrated AI across our business applications and developed specialized scenarios to address specific industry needs. Whether it's ML co-pilots for natural language experiences or AI-powered visual inspections for quality management, our solutions are designed to deliver tangible business outcomes.

Our embed AI scenarios a supplied inside our flagship solution SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP, and Line of Business (LoB) Solutions.

We are also collaborating with our partners with AI-improved applications which are integrated with our cloud solutions.

One of major advantages – since our LoB solutions are integrated with our S/4HANA, so all the all the AI-scenarios we are developing and supplying in LoB are also integrated with our Cloud ERP, and all new capabilities are developed synchronously in all our solutions. All you need to get the latest capabilities and outcomes is to run our cloud solutions.

We are proposing Relevant Scenarios for the Chemical Industry

Building upon customer feedback and industry insights, we have curated a list of AI-powered scenarios tailored for the chemical industry. From customer analysis to demand management and digital assistants, our solutions offer comprehensive support across diverse business domains.

Below is just a couple of examples of AI-powered scenarios we are supplying right now with our and partners’ solutions. Please find couple of examples below which are connected to scenario priorities identified by Chemical communities:

Customer Experience

  • 360 Customer Analysis
  • Mobile Sales assistant
  • AI-powered Price Optimization

Procurement

  • Invoice Conversion
  • Category Recommendation
  • Job description for external contractor workforce

Finance

  • Intelligent Collection
  • Payment Risks Analysis
  • Goods receipt and invoice matching

Supply Chain Management

  • Demand Management
  • Demand-driven replenishment
  • Predictive Shipment Analysis

Manufacturing and Operations

  • Chemical Technology Process non-linear optimization
  • Visual Inspection for Asset and Manufacturing Quality Management
  • Safety Data Sheet Data Extraction

Governance & IT

  • Digital Assistant – Co-pilot for application users
  • Analytics with Natural Language Report Builder
  • Master Data Management
  • Application Custom Code Generation and Analysis

Above is just a list of examples to demonstrate how we are responding on priorities defined by our customers. You can find a lot more information about our AI-powered capabilities in our roadmap. 

Customer Success Stories: Realizing the Potential of AI

Our clients stand as testaments to the transformative power of AI. From optimizing data extraction processes to enhancing production outcomes and improving forecast accuracy, SAP Business AI solutions have catalyzed tangible business growth and operational excellence.

Some of our customers have already realized AI scenarios based on our technologies. Couple of examples are:

  1. Global Chemical company uses Business AI for extraction pertinent data from semi-structured packaging specifications from suppliers and for recording the data into ERP for further processing. It allowed the company to save time and workload
  2. Big Fertilizer Producer is using the ML assistance for technology equipment, which forecasts the optimal process conditions. As a result, the production outcome has been increased on the value from 2 to 10% in tons of production.
  3. Global Petrochemical company uses AI to gather, extract and analyse unstructured external world data to improve forecast accuracy for the commodity prices and demand volumes by regions for their products. They are using these inputs as modifiers for demand process and as a result they have increased their forecast accuracy, sales revenue, and margin
  4. Well known Specialty Chemical company implemented next-generation technologies and artificial intelligence to streamline onboarding for its contact centre representatives and automate the categorization of incoming service tickets, leading to faster onboarding and less time spent on manual tasks

More of examples can be found in our Customer Stories Finder 

AI: How to get there

In conclusion, SAP is at the forefront of driving AI innovation in the chemical industry. With a steadfast commitment to relevance, reliability, and responsibility, we empower businesses to harness the full potential of AI and navigate the complexities of the digital age with confidence.

As a business application vendor, we know well how to integrate AI scenarios into business processes, how to select and score scenarios, how define measure the success, how to define the scenario in detail, how to realize it on our business and technical platform.

If you're interested in learning more about how Business AI can elevate your organization, I invite you to reach out to me and join the conversation. Together, let's build an impressive case powered by SAP Business AI.

  1. SAP Roadmap for Chemicals, with AI  as focus topic of interest
  2. SAP Store - Partner Solutions for Chemical Industry, powered with AI 

Please contact me if you are interested and want more information about positioning of Business AI for the Chemical Industry. Please do nit hesitate give ypur feedbck on it.

Best regards, Sergey Nozhenko, SAP IBU Chemicals

entry.title: material plan price API
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We need to enter plan price to materials as mass upload. We tried to find API but couldn't find. Do you know any API for entering  plan price or standard screen as mass upload ?

Thank you in advance

entry.title: In NWDS when exporting mapping to spreadsheet the constant values are not visible
entry.content:
Hi All,

i am using NWDS mapping Export to spreadsheet and works fine however the constants show up as const() so the actual value is not visible.  What am i doing wrong?
In SapPoConst.jpg you can see part of the export and in SapPoVisual.jpg the actual definition from NWDS mapping visual editor.

Thx Danny.

entry.title: Re: Which SAP GUI for Windows as of release are adapted f...
entry.content: Thanks

entry.title: Where to define the partners list (person responsible inside the work order)
entry.content:
Hello,

I searched in the web but I didn't found any answer to my question. 

Where I can add the list of person responsible that I have to insert inside the person responsible field? Now is empty. I don't know how to have the values to be inserted in that field. 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
Hi @saurabhkumbhare and thank you 🙂

You create your own events as "RAP Business Events". This means in the behavior definition of the BO we can add any kind of event we want our business object to have. Then, instead of a service binding which we do for OData services or regular APIs, we create an Event binding. Here is a full tutorial on our developers tutorial page, going through it step by step: Create RAP Business Events in an On-Premise system

entry.title: Re: WhatsApp Group of Kolkata and Exam
entry.content:
@DataTherapist Thank you for the reply,

I want to about the exam, means everyone of Kolkata's SAP community people have to appear for the exam ? Based on the exam score what will happen means is there any reward or something will be given to them, I did not know about the exam previously, if the exam will happen again I also want to take part, what is the exam is basically for ?

And this there any WhatsApp group for Kolkata people, if it is there can I eligible to join there ?

Thanks and Regards,

Sandip Saha

entry.title: Re: COGI Error with a Neg value: IMSEG-ERFMG is negative. Only positive values can be processed.
entry.content: Hi Caetano, we have same problem too. we can not clean the cogi error (which is = IMSEG-ERFMG is negative. Only positive values can be processed.) . How can we clear it ? --> "is Using this BAPI_PROCORDCONF_GET_TT_PROP an one time effort to clear the error? ".. And for confirmation yes we are using a bapi (z program) and the cogi message is created by it. Would appreciate if you give more detail.

Sathya

entry.title: Re: Broadcast Freight settlement documents charge calcula...
entry.content:
Hello,

Kindly enable "Calculate Charges after Tendering" in Tendering Process Setting in config and try to test.

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: Broadcast Freight settlement documents charge calcula...
Re: SAP BTP: How to call protected app across regions with SAML and OAuth [4]: central custom IdP
entry.content:
Once up and running it's a good system landscape, up to this point it's a tough way to go and should only be considered with a good understanding of BTP infrastructure and SAML2 / OAuth protocols.

We are using it in a productive environment having an IAS as IdP for both sub accounts connected, but different Corporate Identity Providers for each Subaccount to enable direct SSO on both sides without this annoying Screen to put in your E-Mail Address to identify which corporate IdP should be used.

entry.title: Re: Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info S...
Re: Predefined value for Trigger Form in SAP Build Proces...
entry.content:
Hi, have you solved it?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
Hi @mlauber ,

 

You are awesome and thanks for the quick reply.

So one question relating this. We are trying to actually create a z-table in S4HANA (but via using the Custom Business Object App as you mentioned above). Then we would create the RAP Business Events so that SAP Cloud Integration can subscribe to the changes in this table. So creating a Z-table via the Custom Business Object approach be considered as "Clean Core" ?

 

Thanks
Saurabh

entry.title: Allocation from parent to child nodes
entry.content:
Hello,

I have the following scenario:

I have data on a parent node that I need allocated down to its children proportional to data placed on the same measure but on another version that has data both on parent and child level. 

So the -1.000 marked in yelloes needs to be allocated down to its children based on the original distribution through a data action.

Any ideas?

entry.title: Re: How much does the "Process Integration Runtime" BTP service plan cost?
entry.content: This is an interesting conversation however I'm wondering if there is some official documentation from SAP that states the price conditions regarding SAP Process Integration Runtime.

entry.title: Re: Where can delegates access for users be accessed in t...
entry.content: Revising as I accidently added comment as answer.

entry.title: Unable to Get the Product List of Product Group 1 to 5
entry.content:
We are unable to get the product master list of sales view where get the Product group 1 to 5

 

Please help to find how to retrieve this information?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Get the Product List of Product Group 1 to 5
entry.content:
The Product group 1 to 5 can be found using the CDS View I_PRODUCTSALESDELIVERY ,

using the app Export Master Data for the table MVKE and also using the API CE_PRODUCT_0002 and Entity  ProductSalesDelivery

entry.title: How to pass parameter to getEntitySet from SAPUI5?
entry.content:
Hello everyone! I read the following post Solved: How to pass filter parameters to Get EntitySet fro... - SAP Community but it was not helpful. Basically, I need to pass a string as a parameter to my OData Gateway (getEntitySet) to bring a set of data filtered by said string from a Z table. My code for my controller in SAPUI5 is as follows:

 

var oFilterN = new Filter('FilterExample', FilterOperator.EQ, oExample);

var oParams = {
  filters: [oFilterN]
}
var oFilter = "/GetEntitySetExample";

oModel.read(oFilter, oParams, {
   success: function(oData){
      var oDataObtained = oData;
      var oModel = new JSONModel(oData);
      this.getView().setModel(oModel, "modelExample");
   }.bind(this),
    error: function(oError){}

});

 

(In the gateway service, I manage to obtain the parameter with this code, but it never enters the success or error of the controller in sapui5, so it does not allow me to obtain the data set)

And the code for my gateway service is as follows:

 

method getEntitySetExample_get_entityset.
  DATA: my_filter_options type /iwbep/t_mgw_select_option,
  lv_param type ztable-keycamp.
  READ TABLE it_filter_select_options INTO DATA(ls_filter) INDEX 1.
  lv_param = ls_filter-select_options[ 1 ]-low.
  select * from ZTABLE INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE et_entityset WHERE keycamp EQ lv_param.
endmethod.

entry.title: SCC4 monitoring via SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement for setting up the alert for managed system Client Open & Close (SCC4) change settings.

Could you please guide to setup this requirement.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SCC4 monitoring via SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM does not offer the capability to monitor a single Transaction code (such as SCC4) for all activity.

SAP Cloud ALM offers a series of use-cases with different monitoring capabilities.
The closest approximations to your desire are the Configuration & Security Analysis use case and the Real User Monitoring use case.
 
In the Configuration & Security Analysis application you can display the configuration items of the technical configuration using Store Browser. 
The "Changes" view can be used to evaluate alterations to items in time frames. 
The "Administration" view provides information about the managed systems and their status in CSA.
Configuration & Security Analysis does not offer alerting or e-mail notification functionality. 

The Real User Monitoring use case allows the monitoring of Dialog back-end processes.
I lack deep knowledge of the SCC4 Transaction code.
But if it triggers a dialog process then it may be possible to monitor it using the Real User Monitoring app.
Real User Monitoring does offer alerting and e-mail notification functionality. 
But it can only be triggered for very specific circumstances (usually errors).

A combination of using the apps directly and leveraging the available alert and notification functionality may help you to reach an approximation of the monitoring that you desire. 

If you feel that SAP Cloud ALM is not currently meeting your monitoring needs, please refer to the SAP Customer Influence programs for SAP Cloud ALM:
3391676 - SAP Continuous Influence for SAP Cloud ALM

Please review the existing idea requests and vote on any you feel capture you needs.
If you have a specific need not captured in an existing idea, create a new one to inform our Developers of the requirement.

entry.title: Re: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
Hi again @saurabhkumbhare ,

Yes, absolutely that's clean core. In your case, you need to consider if you want to use Custom Business Object, which generates CDS for you, or if you want to build your own RAP Business Object manually, including z-table. Some thoughts:

  • Custom Business Object is fast and simple, but slightly more limited as you can only use the app
    • Pro: fast and easy
    • Con: less flexible. Can't link to existing data elements, such as CustomerNumber etc.

  • Creating RAP BO manually takes time, but if done with ABAP Cloud development version is considered clean core, and is very powerful, as you are free to code. Step-by-step (very fast and simple): z-table via CDS in ADT (not se11). Basic interface view (I_*) to read from z-table. RAP BO data definition(s) to read from Basic interface view (root entities R_*). Data definition projection(s) (C_*). Behavior definition (here you add your business event(s)) for your RAP BO data definition(s) (often the same name R_*). Behavior implementation class (if needed). Behavior definition projection (C_*). Service definition and binding. Event binding.
    • Pro: extremely flexible and powerful - anything is possible really. Can link to existing data elements and sources in z-table and via value help definition in CDS
    • Con: complex. a lot of work. takes time

If you are unsure, you can always try a more simple PoC and start working with both to see which fits your case better.

entry.title: Re: SAP Asset Manager 2110 Now Available – What's New?
entry.content:
Hi,

Are Supervisory Mode and CATS are not possible with 2310 release also?

Best Regards,

Rajendra Pandey

entry.title: SAP PI - Determine the receiver based on Source FileName
entry.content:
Hi all,

Ive an issue regarding receiver determination based on a Source FileName.

Ive a file to file scenario without any ESR objects. 

The FileNames look like this:

CRM_12345_TimeStamp
CRM_<all Other Values>_TimeStamp

Based on the source FileName I need to send Files containing the value 12345 to Receiver 1 and all Files containing other values except 12345 to Receiver 2

I try this in the Iflow:

(ContextObject.FileName CP "*12345*") --> Reiceiver 1  --> This is working

(ContextObject.FileName != "*12345*") --> Reiceiver 2  --> This is NOT working

Ive red that (Not) Equal to with a Wilcard (*) is not working.

So what is the solution here? How can I achieve this and solve this issue?

Your help is appreciated,

Kind regards

John

entry.title: Release Question: Comparing Previous Months
entry.content: If you can help identify which release the commission compare vs previous month(s) will be released? (and any details you can share about it)

entry.title: Re: How much does the "Process Integration Runtime" BTP service plan cost?
entry.content: This is an interesting conversation and clear answer however I'm wondering if there is some official documentation from SAP that states the price conditions regarding SAP Process Integration Runtime. When activating the service you get a popup that this could lead to extra costs. The popup guides you to the service catalog where you find no more information on the topic. As we had already the pleasure of unexpected charging on CPI not in line with the sales talk and not documented in the discovery site (seems on OSS note documented some exceptions to the general rule but you cannot know upfront), I would like to avoid another surprise. Any idea where to find the price statement on SAP Cloud Platform Integration RUNTIME? Many thanks in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 3
Re: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
This is now perfectly clear! We need to advertise this blog a bit more as it shows -

1. The relationship to the old world Z-tables and SM30

2. Clears the air and the misconception around "Z table means NOT clean core"

3. Shows how the new RAP framework enables all of this while still keeping the core clean.

Thank you once again @mlauber .

entry.title: Re: EDI 810 invoice number limitation
entry.content:
Hello,

Invoice number character limits can be set by each buyer in their SAP Business Network account. If you are part of a buying organization, check out this article- 

How can I restrict the maximum number of characters for service sheet and invoice numbers?
 
If you're a supplier wanting to increase the limit of invoice numbers, contact your buyer to discuss. 
 
Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: JDBC (SQL) access for CDS view that uses @ClientHandl...
entry.content:
Sorry for late response!

This was giving me problems too. Managed to find a solution though:

jdbc:sap://ourHanahost:31044/?autocommit=false&sessionVariable:CDS_CLIENT=200

Hope this helps someone else.

yes, we are still using the well known extractors in our S4/BW4 environment... Works 🙂

 

Regards,

Kamil

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content:
Hi @SV2000 
Thank you for posting this very important question.
Would you please repost it to in our Q&A area
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses 
Would you mind to repost it ?
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! 
We are here to support you.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Disclosure Management for EFRAG taxonomy
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

some customers asked us to implement the new EFRAG taxonomy (https://www.efrag.org/Lab4) and show us new functional requirements. There are several differences with respect to old ESEF scenario in SAP DM. In particular:

- every cells or text block could be tagged with more than one dimension

- some cells or block tests should let a short list to report success or not in implement a criteria, for instance.

- SAP DM should be managed (at least) two different taxonomy in the same report.

I read that the last one would be implemented in a new release in Q2 2024. However, will this new version have some functionality to manage the first two new criteria?

Regards,

Andrea

entry.title: WebIDE: Extending Create Purchase Requisition New (F1643A)
entry.content:
We have multiple requirements that requires us to extending the standard app Create Purchase Requisition - New. We created an Adaptation Project of it but we are a bit stuck on how to overwrite methods inside the standard app. We need to overwrite whatever is happening on the Add Source of Supply button. 

From check the original code of the app, it seems like it is actually an extension of the templates (sap.suite.ui.generic.template). And the method that we would like to overwrite is actually on the ext/controller folder in SourceOfSupplyList.controller.js. 

Using controller extension of Adaptation project, our app is triggering onInit and onAfterRender, however it is unable to trigger our onAddFixedSupplier. 

Are we missing something in our code or it is even possible to really extend this application.

entry.content:
Hi all,

Anyone experience with triggering an email after completing a custom task in Onboarding 2.0?

I have created a business to trigger the execute 'Trigger Email Notification for Onboarding Processes'. I have based it on the process object. Also tried to base it on the custom_task object. I see the business rule is triggered and the execution of the trigger is done. But I don't see any mails in the 'Email status' in 'Email Services'. 

As content builder I have used 'Onboarding Custom Task Content Builder' and for the Recipient builder the 'Explicit RecipientBuilder'.

entry.title: Re: How to pass parameter to getEntitySet from SAPUI5?
entry.content:
Hi,

to understand you correct, you just want to use a basic filter like you showed in your provided code?

Regarding your code: 

 

The read function has only two parameters : The path and parameters. (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/api/sap.ui.model.odata.v2.ODataModel%23methods/read)

Check if this code works (assuming oExample is a String):

 

var oFilterN = new Filter('FilterExample', FilterOperator.EQ, oExample);

var sPath = "/GetEntitySetExample";

oModel.read(sPath, {
   filters: [oFilterN],
   success: function(oData){
      var oDataObtained = oData;
      var oModel = new JSONModel(oData);
      this.getView().setModel(oModel, "modelExample");
   }.bind(this),
    error: function(oError){}

});

The new value would be the output of the form.

Or is there any other function for that?

Basically, I want to give the user a chance to change the value, e.g. of the amount.

SAP Build SAP Build Process Automation SAP Business Technology Platform 

I guess its an easy one for you @Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance - 'Colombia' - Contingency Process
entry.content:
This blog offers insights into the contingency process feature within the SAP DRC Colombia solution and details the steps required for setting it up.

Occasionally, users encounter difficulties submitting eDocuments to the Colombian tax authorities (DIAN) owing to technical glitches within the system or communication errors. These issues may arise from the electronic bill issuer's end or from DIAN itself, resulting in the unsuccessful submission of eDocuments.

The different types of contingencies are distinguished based on the '<InvoiceTypeCode>' tag. The value 03 represents contingency due to technical issues on the electronic bill issuer's side, and 04 represents contingency due to technical issues on DIAN.

 

DIAN Contingency - Tipo - 04

Technological problems generated by DIAN for the prior validation of electronic invoicing. Electronic billers can set up this contingency process when one of the following error messages are received from DIAN.

  1. Error Handling: If an error (specifically mentioned in section 12.2.1) is detected when transmitting an electronic sales invoice, the invoice must be re-transmitted to DIAN 20 seconds after the error is detected. If the error persists after three attempts (one minute after the first attempt), the invoice should be issued directly to the buyer without prior validation from DIAN.

  2. Error Documentation: Providers must maintain records of the errors encountered as specified in section 12.2.1.

    Figure 1.1 - Possible error codes during DIAN Contingency

    Follow the link to DIAN documentation for more information.

  3. Invoice Generation with Errors: In case of persistent technological issues, invoices should be issued with an InvoiceTypeCode of 04, maintaining the same prefix and number, and should be re-signed and delivered without DIAN’s validation response.

  4. Monitoring DIAN Services: Providers should monitor DIAN’s electronic billing web services 30 minutes after an error is first reported to check if the service has been restored. Monitoring should continue until the service is fully operational.

  5. Post-Recovery Actions: Once the DIAN service is active again, providers should generate, transmit, and issue electronic documents normally.

  6. Reporting Timeline: Electronic billers have 48 hours to report to DIAN any invoices issued due to technological issues once they verify that DIAN's services are operational.

 

Figure 1.2 - Provides context about the steps to be followed in case of DIAN Contingency

 

 

Electronic Bill Issuer Contingency - Tipo -03 

Technological problems at electronic bill issuer system. If a technical problem prevents you from sending eDocuments to DIAN for a long period of time, you define in Customizing that all eDocuments are created in contingency mode.

The steps outline procedures for electronic billers to follow during technological issues in the issuer system:

  1. Issuing Invoices During Contingency: If there are technological problems, billers should issue sales invoices manually or through electronic systems, using either booklet or paper formats.

  2. Communication of Issues: Billers must draft a letter detailing the technological issue or its resolution, signed by the company’s legal representative. This letter should be sent to a specified email address, with certain required details in the subject, attachment, and body of the email.

  3. Post-Issue Procedures: Once the technological issue is resolved, billers should resume normal operations of generating, transmitting, issuing, and receiving electronic documents.

  4. Documentation and Notification: During the contingency period, information from manually issued invoices must be transcribed into an electronic transmission document. Notifications of acceptance or rejection from DIAN (the tax authority) will follow within 48 hours of resolving the issue.

  5. Specific Documents Without Contingency Plans: CreditNote, DebitNote, and ApplicationResponse documents should not use contingency numbering but instead follow the established numbering by the biller.

For further information about this type of contingency, please refer to the DIAN documentation link.

Figure 1.3- Provides information about the contingency due to Electronic biller's system issues

 SDC Colombia Contingency Process setup:

 

When an eDocument submitted from edoc cockpit and if it results in one of the below statuses, then the documents are must be processed in DIAN contingency mode (InvoiceTypeCode - 04) / Electronic Bill Issuer contingency mode (InvoiceTypeCode - 03) :

Communication Exception Status in EDOC Cockpit

This situation occurs when there is a communication issue at DIAN, and the document does not reach its system. You can perform the following steps to complete the process:

  1. Validate if the error messages received from DIAN is one of the following

If you are not able to see the error code in the application log or interface log in the cockpit, you must check it in the CPI logs; here is an example screenshot-

 

 

If you receive the message "Time out" while transmitting electronic documents, it indicates a delay in the DIAN System. In such a situation, you must treat it as a DIAN contingency with an InvoiceTypeCode of 04. If the same issue persists after five attempts, then you should still consider it as a DIAN contingency of the same type.

 Steps to be followed for DIAN Contingency mode:

  1. Select the eDocument with the status 'Communication Exception' and choose the 'Switch to Contingency' action
  2. Choose Yes in the next dialog box.
  3. The status changes to 'Switched to DIAN Contingency'
  4. Send an electronic invoice to the customer without validations from DIAN
  5. Select eDocument and choose Customer-->Send to Customer option
    1. The system allows sending email to customer in this situation; you can confirm this in the History section
    2. Once the technological issues are resolved on DIANs side you must submit the edocument again and DIAN performs the validations, Approves and Rejects the edocuments.

The resubmitted document must have the InvoiceTypeCode 04. The History section of the edocument can show this.

 

Sending to DIAN requested

There is a technical issue in the electronic invoice issuer system, and the eDocument cannot be sent to DIAN. If the issue cannot be resolved quickly, you can switch the eDocument to contingency. After that, you manually reverse/cancel the source document related to this eDocument in the source application.

Note:

  • If the issue is related to data and can be resolved easily, switching to the contingency mode is unnecessary. Correct the data and resubmit the data again.
  • You must have prenumbered forms approved by DIAN

 Steps to be followed for Contingency at the invoice issuer's System:

Once you identify the technological issues in the system that cannot be solved quickly, follow the steps to create eDocuments in contingency mode (Electronic bill issuer contingency - 03)

  1. Choose the eDocument with the status 'Sending to DIAN Requested' and select 'Switch to Contingency' action
  2. Choose Yes in the next dialog.
  3. Status of the eDocument changes to 'Switched to Contingency'
  4. Cancel the Invoice
  5. Perform the configuration to allow the creation of the eDocuments in contingency mode
  6. In the source applications, you create new documents for the eDocument you have switched to contingency. Before posting the documents, you enter the number of the prenumbered document you used for the contingency documents:
    1. For FI documents, you enter the number of the prenumbered form in the Reference Number field of the new source document.
    2. For SD documents, you enter the number of the prenumbered form in the Reference field of the new source document when you are creating the sales order (VA01 transaction) 
    3. Example:
  7. The newly created eDocument will be seen in the 'Colombia eInvoice Contingency' process in cockpit
  8. Once the technological issues are resolved, submit the eDocuments
    1. Note: The InvoiceTypeCode will be 03.
  9. Reverse the contingency configuration to allow the creation of the eDocuments normally
  10. After reversal, the configuration appears as below:

 

In summary, effectively managing the contingency processes within the SAP DRC Colombia solution is crucial for maintaining compliance with DIAN regulations during technological disruptions. This blog has explored the necessary steps and considerations for setting up and navigating through contingency modes, whether the challenges arise from the electronic bill issuer's end or directly from DIAN.

Understanding the differentiation between 'InvoiceTypeCode' 03 and 04 helps in pinpointing the origin of the issue and deciding the appropriate response strategy. The outlined procedures ensure that, even in the face of technological difficulties, businesses can continue their operations by issuing invoices in contingency modes and maintaining necessary documentation for compliance and verification.

As technology evolves and challenges emerge, staying informed and prepared to handle such issues with the appropriate contingency plans will safeguard the integrity of electronic document transactions and support uninterrupted business operations.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere DBADMIN and Data provisioning agent is...
entry.content:
Hi T. Durham,

I don't think you need to connect the DB User from the Space Administration page to the provisioning agent.

If your purpose is to configure DP Agent for Datasphere, you may follow:

  • Administering SAP Datasphere > Install the Data Provisioning Agent
  • Connect and Configure the Data Provisioning Agent
  • SAP Data Warehouse Cloud - DP agent set up and configuration by @Hemanth1 

Regards,
Tuncay

you should be able to workaround the issue by replacing fiori run with ui5 serve, until a fix for the issue is provided.

Zdravko

entry.title: How to update SOW workers roles

There is a default role assigned to all the SOW workers but I wish to update role based on what system access they should have.

For example, a project manager role is added in SOW and I would like to change it to other text in the SOW when I am adding the SOW worker.

SAP Fieldglass Vendor Management System

Product Valuation Costing

Narayanan.

entry.title: Re: Where to define the partners list (person responsible...
entry.content:
Hi,

Where you have inserted the Person Responsible ? Are you expecting the person assigned in Work Centre ?

The field which you have highlighted flows from HR search, not from work centre assignment

Regards

Terence

entry.title: Re: SAC Story Filters: Advanced Filtering with BW Live Data
entry.content:
Hi Martin,

It's not about  classic stories or optimized stories! I don't think Advanced Filtering works with Live Data Models. It works only for Import and Planning Data Models. Please check out: 

  • SAC Story Filters: Advanced Filtering with Live Data
  • SAP Note 2788384 Unsupported Features with SAP BW, SAP S/4HANA and SAP BPC Embedded Model Live Connections in SAC

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Referral code for Adspower: MjUI2ExqV8XLvrw (Get the best bonus for signing up)
entry.content:
The code for AdsPower is MjUI2ExqV8XLvrw. With this one-of-a-kind code, you can get 40% off all AdsPower orders and subscriptions. Plus, if you share this special reference code, you could get 50% cashback.

entry.title: Scheduling parameters usage & what is forward scheduling & backward scheduling in maintenance orders
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

I have doubts regarding the topic Scheduling parameters while configuring the maintenance order types.

What are Scheduling parameters & what is forward scheduling & backward scheduling in maintenance orders.

What is the meaning of scheduling type.

What is the meaning of production supervisor *, while doing the configuration of maintenance order scheduling setting.

What is the difference between automatic scheduling, automatic log, scheduling with breaks, shift order, latest staging date.

 

Regards,

Arif

entry.title: Re: Where to define the partners list (person responsible...
entry.content:
Hello, nope, im not searching for HR linking inside the work centers...im searching for the partners. If you see, under that field, there is the PARNR element. 

I would to know ho to create the list of partners in order to see those within that field, "person responsbile". 

That screenshot is the work order, not the work center

Thanks

entry.title: Partitioning a table in HANA using Range Partitioning using multiple values from Partitioning column
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am planning to perform a single level Range Partitioning of a table in HANA.

As per the example given in SAP documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/HANA_SERVICE_CF/6a504812672d48ba865f4f4b268a881e/c338627bbb571014ae7d9533861b600f.html) I can create a single partition using only one VALUE from the partitioning column.

Example: PARTITION BY RANGE("TESTRESULT") ((PARTITION VALUES = 0), (PARTITION VALUES = -1), (PARTITION VALUES = -10), (PARTITION OTHERS));

Is it possible to create a single partition by using more than one values from the partitioning column? 

To explain my question further using the same example above, can I create one partition using VALUES '0' and '-1' from the column TESTRESULT?

Thanks,

Shuvendu

entry.title: Ariba Supplier Registration status did not change to pending resubmit
entry.content:
Hello,  we have a supplier that was previously registered and approved.  They made changes to their registration.  We responded needing additional information and updates to the registration. 

The log shows details:  Supplier registration project registration update status changed from Pending Approval to Pending Resubmit.

When we look at the supplier registration there is an information message at the top:  The registration questionnaire is pending resubmission from the supplier.   However, the Registration Status still shows as Registered.  We were expecting this to change to Pending Resubmit.

Is this a correct status/behavior since the supplier was previously registered and approved?  Should the status have changed?  If so, where might I look for any missing logic of configuration settings?

Thank you,  Cheryl
